headline: Early Childhood Education Only Reaches 72% of B40 Children
context: UNICEF Malaysia's 2025 report found significant preschool enrollment gaps among low-income families.

cards.big: 28% of B40 children aged 4-5 are not enrolled in any preschool program
cards.sub: National average enrollment is 87% but gap widens for poorest quintile

cards.big: Government KEMAS preschools charge RM2.50 monthly but only have 12,800 classes
cards.sub: Demand exceeds capacity by an estimated 35,000 places nationwide

cards.big: Nobel laureate James Heckman calculated 13% annual return on early childhood investment
cards.sub: Every RM1 spent on quality preschool saves RM7 in later social costs

cards.big: 4 separate agencies run preschool programs with different curricula and standards
cards.sub: KEMAS, PERPADUAN, MOE, and private providers lack unified quality framework

cards.big: Preschool access is not childcare—it is the foundation of lifetime achievement
Should Malaysia guarantee free universal preschool for all 4-year-olds
